steps.title: Identify the product
steps.body: Find $\displaystyle\int x\cos(x)\,\mathrm{d}x$. This is a product of an algebraic factor $x$ and a trigonometric factor $\cos(x)$, so integration by parts is the right technique.

steps.title: Choose u and dv/dx
steps.body: Let $u = x$ (algebraic — differentiating it simplifies things) and $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}v}{\mathrm{d}x} = \cos(x)$. Then $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x} = 1$ and $v = \sin(x)$.

steps.title: Check by differentiating back
steps.body: Using the product rule: $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d}x}\big(x\sin(x) + \cos(x)\big) = \big(\sin(x) + x\cos(x)\big) - \sin(x) = x\cos(x)$ — matches the original integrand. ✓
